title: quercus cerris subsp. austriaca
intro: Quercus cerris subsp. austriaca, also known as the Austrian turkey oak or Austro-Slovenian Turkey oak, is a subspecies of the Quercus cerris tree, native to the mountainous regions of the Balkans and Central Europe. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Quercus cerris subsp. austriaca include the potential to reduce inflammation and protect against oxidative stress, making it a promising agent in the treatment of various health conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
